query: Rivian R2 base trim price under $60000 horsepower specs all trim levels March 2026 lineup
a_issues: Result snippets are somewhat inconsistent on base pricing/timing: Edmunds says starting price around $45,000, while MotorTrend/KBB quote $46,495 including destination and say the cheapest trim arrives later. Automotive World and EV Report snippets say $48,490 for Standard, which conflicts with the official Rivian site’s $44,990 starting price shown in the anchor. The official Rivian result snippet is also sparse and does not expose horsepower directly.
b_issues: Contains less complete coverage of all trim-level horsepower. Automotive World and Edmunds only provide launch/performance pricing and 656 hp. The official Rivian newsroom snippet is largely irrelevant boilerplate and omits the key trim specs. Car and Driver says initial R2s ship as Premium 450 hp or Performance 656 hp, but there is no clear base-trim horsepower in the B set except via MotorTrend. Pricing is also inconsistent: $57,990 MSRP / $59,485 with destination / $48,490 start, and the under-$60k base-trim interpretation is less clear than in A.
reasoning: A better enables a correct answer because it more directly supplies the key facts the question asks for: that the lineup is under $60,000 at launch and ultimately has a much lower base price, plus horsepower across trims. In A, MotorTrend gives all three horsepower figures: Standard 350 hp, Premium 450 hp, Performance 656 hp. KBB reinforces Performance at $59,485 including destination and explicitly notes the low-end price reaches $46,495, while Edmunds adds the widely cited ~$45,000 starting point. Although A has some pricing inconsistencies and timing ambiguity, it still gives enough to reconstruct a mostly correct answer. B has the same pricing inconsistencies but weaker trim coverage overall: only MotorTrend clearly provides the base and premium horsepower, while other snippets focus mostly on Performance or generic launch details. So A is the stronger set for answering the question correctly from the provided content alone.
